Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Forum Non Conveniens
Off Calendar May-26-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT LYFT, INC.'s Motion To Dismiss For Forum Non Conveniens

APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
John Metri, Esq. (213.389.3765) of Downtown LA Law Group for Plaintiffs.
Cara Sandberg, Esq. (415.343.7100; csandberg@conmetkane.com) of Conrad Metlitzky Kane LLP for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Lyft, Inc.'s "motion to dismiss or stay based on forum non conveniens" is denied.

Plaintiffs Austin Cole Smith and Joana Sultan Smith allege the former was punched by a Lyft driver in a November 2019 dispute over a ride in Arizona. Defendant bears the burden of proof on a non conveniens motion, "meaning defendant must provide the trial court with sufficient evidence to enable it to carry out its weighing and balancing analysis." (Edmon and Karnow, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2016) 3:408.5, citing National Football League v. Fireman's Fund Insurance Co. (2013) 216 Cal.App.4th 902, 926-27.) This Lyft fails to do.

Lyft and the Smiths now all apparently reside in California. (Opp. 2:17-20.) No evidence is provided as to where Lyft driver "Dean Doe" now resides - or even if that is his real name.

Lyft says "any" eyewitnesses, investigators or medical professionals are in Arizona, but provides no identifying information or any other evidence. Lyft says plaintiffs' family, friends and work contacts are in Arizona, but again provides no identifying information or any other evidence.

Lyft likewise says Arizona and California laws "conflict," but identifies no actual conflict. And Lyft says our court has "congested calendars" with no evidence.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Jane Hong, CSR# 11975 (909-831-2644; csrjane@hotmail.com), Reported. =(302/RBU)
